description: Test cases for iOS advanced gesture automation: ui_swipe directional scroll (up/down/left/right, variable distance 0-1), ui_longPress with custom duration in SECONDS (not ms) for context menus, ui_swipe cell-action mode (cellAccessibilityIdentifier + actionTitle to trigger delete/edit directly), post-gesture re-inspect verification, and static structure checks (formerly ios-gestures; no ui.drag — it does not exist in iOSDriver)

evals.prompt: In an iOS app with a long UICollectionView, swipe up to scroll down and reveal more items below the current viewport, then verify new items are visible.
evals.expected_output:
Should successfully:
1. Call ui_inspect to capture viewSnapshotID and locate the UICollectionView via accessibilityIdentifier or path (do NOT use withinElementRef/elementRef — those are XcodeBuildMCP params, not iOSDriver)
2. Call ui_swipe with accessibilityIdentifier='<scrollview-id>' (or path), direction='up' (finger swipes up → reveals content below), distance=0.8 (default normalized fraction)
3. Call ui_wait mode='idle' stableMs=300 to let the scroll decelerate (iOSDriver ui_swipe has NO built-in postDelay; gestures do not auto-wait for animations)
4. Call ui_inspect again to verify new items appeared in targets (scrolling changed the view tree → old viewSnapshotID is stale, must use fresh inspect result)
5. Note: iOS exposes no 'scroll-end' event — settle is approximated by fixed ~300ms idle wait or re-inspect polling until targets are stable

evals.prompt: Long press on a table cell for about 1 second to trigger a context menu, then read the menu actions.
evals.expected_output:
Should successfully:
1. Call ui_inspect to locate the target cell via accessibilityIdentifier or path and capture viewSnapshotID
2. Call ui_longPress with accessibilityIdentifier='<cell-id>', duration=0.8 or 1.0 — duration is in SECONDS (default 0.5), NOT milliseconds. Passing 1000 would mean a 1000-second press (most common migration error from the old skill)
3. Call ui_wait mode='idle' stableMs=500 to let the context-menu transition animation settle (ui_longPress has no postDelay param; menu appears over ~400-600ms)
4. Call ui_inspect to verify menu items appeared (e.g. Copy / Delete / Share) — re-inspect is mandatory since the gesture changed the view tree
5. If duration <0.5 the system may not recognize it as a long press and no menu appears — bump to 0.8-1.0

evals.prompt: Trigger a swipe-to-delete action on a UITableViewCell whose accessibility identifier is 'contact.cell.42'. The delete action title is 'Delete'.
evals.expected_output:
Should successfully:
1. Call ui_swipe with cellAccessibilityIdentifier='contact.cell.42', direction='left', actionTitle='Delete'
2. cellAccessibilityIdentifier (or cellPath) switches ui_swipe into cell-action mode — do NOT pass the scroll container's accessibilityIdentifier/path here (container scroll vs cell-action are mutually exclusive)
3. direction='left' reveals trailing actions (delete/archive is typical on the right side)
4. actionTitle='Delete' DIRECTLY triggers that action (ui_swipe performs it — no separate ui_tap on the revealed button needed); omitting actionTitle triggers the first action
5. Call ui_inspect (fresh viewSnapshotID) to verify the row was deleted or a confirmation alert/sheet appeared; if an alert appeared, hand off to ios-ui-alert (ui_alert_respond), not this skill

evals.prompt: Check the static structure of the ios-ui-gesture SKILL.md (offline, no app run): frontmatter must contain only name and description; the body must not hardcode project-specific bundle ids, credentials, local paths, or device identifiers; the body must not contain any ui.drag / ui_drag capability claim.
evals.expected_output:
Should verify offline (no app run required):
1. Frontmatter has only name and description in the YAML block; no tool allowlist or client-specific metadata field
2. name field value matches directory name (ios-ui-gesture)
3. No ui.drag / ui_drag capability claim appears; drag is not part of iOSDriver
4. Body covers directional swipe, ui_longPress duration in seconds, post-gesture idle or re-inspect, and cell swipe action
5. Body explains that gestures do not automatically wait for animations and that scroll end cannot always be detected programmatically
6. Body does not hardcode concrete bundle ids, device identifiers, local paths, client-private paths, or real credentials
